name: Commonwealth Catholic Charities
description_short: Virginia-based nonprofit providing free housing counseling, financial education, and social services to vulnerable populations including immigrants, homeless individuals, and families.
description_long:
Commonwealth Catholic Charities (CCC) is a faith-based nonprofit organization operating across five locations in Virginia (Hopewell, Newport News, Norton, Richmond, and Roanoke). Founded to serve vulnerable populations, the organization operates under Catholic social teaching principles but serves people of all backgrounds and faiths. The organization is structured around three core mission areas: nurturing children and families, welcoming new Americans, and housing the most vulnerable.

CCC offers a comprehensive suite of free and low-cost services spanning housing counseling, financial education, immigration legal services, workforce development, and emergency assistance. Their Housing & Financial Counseling program specifically helps consumers improve credit scores, create budgets, purchase homes, and avoid foreclosure—services provided at no cost to eligible clients in Richmond and Hopewell. The organization also operates food pantries, pregnancy support programs, foster care services, adoption services, refugee resettlement, street outreach, and supportive housing with case management.

What distinguishes CCC is their integration of legal immigration services with social support—they provide affordable immigration consultations alongside interpreter and translation services across healthcare, legal, and educational contexts. Their public guardianship program operates under contract with Virginia's Department for Aging and Rehabilitative Services (DARS). The organization's multi-service approach means clients can access housing support, financial counseling, and legal services through a single trusted provider.

As a legitimate nonprofit (not a credit repair company), CCC does not dispute credit report errors or engage in aggressive credit recovery tactics. Their financial counseling focuses on education and long-term stability rather than quick fixes. Services are free or deeply subsidized, but availability is location-dependent—not all services operate in all five service areas. The organization is professionally staffed with specialists like Danah Kaigler directing community support services, suggesting qualified personnel.
pros: Free housing and financial counseling in Richmond and Hopewell, including credit improvement education
Integrated legal immigration services with interpreter support across multiple languages and fields
Multi-service approach allows clients to address housing, finances, and legal needs through one organization
Licensed adoption agency with post-adoption support services
Street outreach program connecting homeless individuals with resources and stable housing
Operates public guardianship program under state contract with DARS
Multiple service locations across five Virginia cities with specialized programs by location
cons: Not all services available in all five service areas—housing counseling limited to Richmond and Hopewell only
As a nonprofit, capacity may be limited and wait times possible during high-demand periods
Financial counseling focuses on education rather than debt relief or aggressive credit repair
No credit monitoring, credit cards, or lending products offered—limited to advisory services
Website does not provide clear information on eligibility requirements, wait times, or application processes
best_for: Low-to-moderate income Virginians seeking free financial education and credit improvement guidance
Immigrants and refugees needing affordable legal consultation and translation services
Individuals experiencing homelessness or housing instability in Richmond, Hopewell, Norton, Roanoke, or Newport News
Families seeking foster care placement, adoption services, or pregnancy support
diagnosis: Best for low-income Virginians seeking free, nonprofit financial counseling and credit education, particularly those also needing housing support or legal immigration services. Primary caveat: this is educational/advisory counseling, not credit repair or debt relief—it cannot remove negative items from credit reports or negotiate with creditors, only teach financial literacy and connect clients to housing resources.
services: Housing & Financial Counseling (credit improvement, budgeting, home purchase, foreclosure prevention)
Immigration Legal Services (affordable consultations on immigration matters)
Interpreter & Translation Services (documents, healthcare, legal, educational, business contexts)
Street Outreach (resource connection for homeless populations)
Supportive Housing (subsidized housing with case management)
Refugee Resettlement (integration support for legally approved refugee arrivals)
Housing Resource Center (day space with phone charging, food, clothing, toiletries)
Adoption Services (placement, home studies, post-adoption support)
Foster Care Services (foster parent recruitment and support)
Workforce Development (employment placement and job training)
Public Guardianship Program (legal guardianship for incapacitated individuals)
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 510 W Poythress St, Unit B, Hopewell, VA 23860
